observations.note: ITERATION 5 PRODUCED NO PERSISTED PRODUCT CODE. J-15 (global-history warm start) was built and validated in an ephemeral copy — apps/backend/.pytest_cache/v/cache/nodeids lists all 8 spec'd J-15 tests and lastfailed holds ONLY the pre-existing red (test_directions_cache), proving the J-15 tests ran GREEN — but the implementation was NEVER merged into this working tree and is NOT recoverable from git. This is the framework's `isolation: worktree` failure mode: work built in a worktree, tested, then the worktree removed without committing/merging back. Independent verification: grep over apps/ finds zero history_scope/mine_history_families/history_planner.py; status.json frozen at current_step:'starting' changed_files:[] tests_run:false; NO dev/review/qa/audit/ui-test-results artifacts exist; git diff HEAD -- apps/ is EMPTY (so J-01..J-14 cannot have regressed); HEAD still iter-4 9e977ab; iter-5 snapshot ccc06b2 = iter-4 + one telemetry line; the only history_scope in dangling commits is the abandoned auto-money-printer session (different lineage). VERDICT CONTINUE (not REGRESSION: nothing passing broke; not STALLED: first no-progress iter + crystal-clear next step). REQUIRED FIX next dispatch: rebuild J-15 from the intact iter-5 spec AND verify it persisted (git diff HEAD -- apps/ shows apps/backend/** changes + dev handoff present + status.json.changed_files non-empty) BEFORE declaring done. A green pytest cache is NOT evidence the code landed in the tree. STALL RISK: if the harness loses the next iteration's work the same way, that WOULD trip a stall — the persistence check is load-bearing.
